title: Missing Standardized Error Response Schema
description: Error responses do not follow a standardized format (RFC 7807 Problem Details), making it difficult for API consumers to handle errors consistently.
check_pattern: error responses missing type, title, status, detail fields, no reusable error schema component
fix_guidance: Define a reusable Error schema in components/schemas following RFC 7807: include type (URI), title (string), status (integer), detail (string), and instance (URI) fields.

title: Missing 401 Unauthorized Response
description: Authenticated endpoints do not define a 401 Unauthorized response, leaving consumers without documentation on authentication failure handling.
check_pattern: endpoints with security requirements missing 401 response definition
fix_guidance: Add a 401 Unauthorized response to all endpoints that have security requirements. Reference a reusable error schema component.

title: Missing 403 Forbidden Response
description: Endpoints with role or scope restrictions do not define a 403 Forbidden response, leaving consumers without documentation on authorization failure.
check_pattern: endpoints with scope-based security missing 403 response, admin endpoints without 403 definition
fix_guidance: Add a 403 Forbidden response to all endpoints with role or scope restrictions. Use a reusable error schema referencing the components section.

title: Missing 429 Too Many Requests Response
description: Endpoints do not document rate limiting behavior via a 429 response, leaving consumers unaware of throttling constraints.
check_pattern: endpoints missing 429 response, no rate limit documentation
fix_guidance: Add a 429 Too Many Requests response to all endpoints. Include Retry-After header documentation. Reference rate limit headers in the response headers section.

title: Inconsistent Error Response Structure
description: Different endpoints return error responses with different structures, making consistent client-side error handling impossible.
check_pattern: varying error response schemas across endpoints, inline error schemas not using shared component
fix_guidance: Create a single Error schema component and reference it from all error responses using $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'.

title: Missing Deprecation Metadata
description: Endpoints that are being phased out do not include the deprecated flag, leaving consumers unaware of upcoming breaking changes.
check_pattern: legacy endpoints without deprecated: true marker, no sunset date in description
fix_guidance: Mark deprecated endpoints with deprecated: true. Include a sunset date and migration path in the operation description. Add a Sunset response header definition.

title: Missing Contact and License Information
description: The API info section lacks contact details and license information, making it difficult for consumers to reach the API team or understand usage rights.
check_pattern: info section missing contact object, missing license object, missing terms of service
fix_guidance: Add contact information (name, email, URL) and license details to the info section. Include termsOfService URL if applicable.

title: Missing Changelog or Version History
description: The API specification does not document version history or changelog, preventing consumers from understanding what changed between versions.
check_pattern: info description missing changelog, no x-changelog extension defined
fix_guidance: Include a changelog section in the info.description field or use the x-changelog OpenAPI extension to document breaking and non-breaking changes per version.

title: Missing Endpoint Summaries and Descriptions
description: Operations lack summary and description fields, making API documentation incomplete and forcing consumers to infer behavior from schema alone.
check_pattern: operations missing summary field, endpoints without description
fix_guidance: Add a concise summary (under 120 characters) and a detailed description to every operation. The description should explain behavior, side effects, and any important constraints.

title: Missing Request and Response Examples
description: Operations and schemas lack examples, making it harder for consumers to understand expected data formats and test integrations.
check_pattern: schemas missing example or examples field, operations without request body examples
fix_guidance: Add example values to all schema properties. Include full request body examples using the examples field. Add response examples for 200, 201, and error responses.

title: Missing Schema Property Descriptions
description: Schema properties lack description fields, leaving consumers to guess the meaning, format, and constraints of data fields.
check_pattern: schema properties missing description, undocumented enum values, missing format specifiers
fix_guidance: Add description fields to all schema properties. Document enum values with their meaning. Use format specifiers (date-time, email, uuid, uri) where applicable.
